jokes.text: Jerry, Jerry, Jerry... I'm so sick of hearing about Jerry, man. Everything is 'Jerry' out of you mouth.
jokes.setup: Character complaining about someone named Jerry being mentioned constantly
jokes.punchline: Jerry is revealed to be Frank's tapeworm
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - audience assumes Jerry is a person until the reveal. Strong character work showing Charlie's jealousy.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Jerry, Jerry, Jerry... I'm so sick of hearing about Jerry, man. Everything is 'Jerry' out of you mouth.

jokes.text: Like, if Jerry jumped off a bridge would you jump off it, too? - Which bridge?
jokes.setup: Charlie uses the classic rhetorical question about following someone off a bridge
jokes.punchline: Frank treats it literally and asks which specific bridge
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor showing Frank's literal interpretation of rhetorical questions.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Like, if Jerry jumped off a bridge would you jump off it, too?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Which bridge?

jokes.text: My tapeworm. - You have a tapeworm? - Yeah, he got a worm. - Yeah. Got a tapeworm. And you named it? - Jerry. - Jerry.

jokes.explanation: The casual delivery of something deeply disturbing is peak Sunny humor. Great character work.

dialogue.line: See, this is the problem. You guys all think that work is tricking people. It's not. It's just your job.

jokes.text: It shifts. I've seen it shift.
jokes.setup: Discussion about the mysterious yuck puddle
jokes.punchline: Mac's disturbed observation that it appears to be alive

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: It shifts. I've seen it shift.

jokes.text: Can I be honest, dude? I think this thing's alive. Oh, that must be what all the bubbling's about, 'cause it's, like, trying to communicate. It definitely feeds. I-I've found bones in it, man.

jokes.punchline: Charlie escalates with increasingly horrific details
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from suspicion to body horror. Great character work showing their casual acceptance of the impossible.

dialogue.line: Oh, that must be what all the bubbling's about, 'cause it's, like, trying to communicate. It definitely feeds. I-I've found bones in it, man.

jokes.punchline: Mac's complete lack of self-awareness about his obvious signs

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Well, he may have suspected it before, but there's no way he could have known for sure. 'Cause I show no outward signs of being gay, and that's just the end of the story, all right?
